title: Which Sandra Bullock Character Would You Actually Be In A Crisis?
dek: Eight roles, eight completely different survival instincts — answer honestly and find out if you're a Gracie, a Ryan, or a full-blown Malorie.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just pick whatever your gut says first.

questions.question: The building alarm just went off. What's your first move?

answers.text: Crack a joke to calm everyone down, then take charge like it's just another Tuesday.
Adrenaline takes over — I'm acting before I've even finished processing what's happening.
I freeze for a beat, then quietly do the brave thing because nobody else is.
I go completely silent and methodical, running through the checklist in my head.
Bonding with a total stranger under insane pressure and somehow it just... works.
Falling for the version of someone I built up in my head, then getting surprised by who's actually real.
Trust built in total silence — someone who has my back with zero need for words.
Loud, unconditional, take-you-in-as-family love, no questions asked.

questions.question: How would your coworkers describe your ambition?

answers.text: Quietly dreaming way bigger than my day job while I punch the clock.
Driven to the point of tunnel vision — I need to finish what I started.
A force of nature. Once I decide something needs to happen, it happens.
Singularly focused on one goal and nothing else matters until it's done.
Total, blissful silence. No noise, no people, just quiet.
Running the whole household like a general while somehow also baking something.
Doors locked, blinds shut, staying alert even when nothing's wrong.
Sketching out a plan for something I probably shouldn't be planning.

questions.question: Your boss just gave you an insane, borderline unfair assignment. You...

answers.text: Push back immediately and negotiate the terms on my own.
Assume it's a trap and prepare for the worst-case scenario anyway.
Turn it into an opportunity and quietly recruit allies to help pull it off.
Take it over completely and make it clear who's actually in charge now.

questions.question: What's your idea of a perfect team?

answers.text: As small as possible. Fewer people, fewer ways to lose someone.
A hand-picked crew where everyone has exactly one irreplaceable skill.
One that runs like a machine because I refuse to work with amateurs.
One where nobody takes themselves too seriously and everybody still gets the job done.

questions.question: How do you handle being humiliated in public?

answers.text: Play it cool, like it was part of the plan all along.
Get icy, professional, and pretend it never happened.
Laugh it off — loudly, awkwardly, and completely unbothered.
Push through it. There's no time to be embarrassed when there's a bigger problem at hand.
Being seen as soft. Vulnerability terrifies me more than failure.
Being fundamentally unlovable once people see the 'real' me.
Freezing up at the exact moment it matters most.
Staying invisible forever, never getting the bigger life I secretly want.

questions.question: How do you show someone you actually care about them?

answers.text: By showing up for them even when I clearly don't want to be there.
By staying calm and steady for them when everything else is falling apart.
By quietly doing the brave, selfless thing without expecting credit.
By finally letting them see the parts of me I usually hide.

questions.question: Pick the ending that feels most like you.

answers.text: Walking away from the chaos a little shaken, but stronger for it.
Getting the honest, real love story I didn't even know I needed.
Coming back from the brink, grateful just to feel solid ground again.
Watching the person I fought for finally succeed because of it.

results.name: Gracie Hart
results.subtitle: Miss Congeniality (2000)
results.description: You're the undercover overachiever — self-deprecating, a little clumsy, allergic to feminine expectations, but somehow always the one who ends up saving the day and winning everyone over anyway. You'd rather crack a joke than admit you're trying hard, but you're trying really hard.

results.name: Annie Porter
results.subtitle: Speed (1994)
results.description: You're scared, you'll admit it, and you keep going anyway. You don't need to be fearless to be the one who holds it together when it matters — you just need to act like you know what you're doing until you actually do.

results.name: Lucy Moderatz
results.subtitle: While You Were Sleeping (1995)
results.description: You've built entire fantasy lives in your head about people you barely know, and honestly, it's kind of romantic. You're lonelier than you let on, but you're also the one who jumps onto the train tracks without a second thought when someone actually needs you.

results.name: Dr. Ryan Stone
results.subtitle: Gravity (2013)
results.description: You cope by burying yourself in the work and building walls nobody can get through — until you're forced into total isolation and have to find the will to fight your way back. You're not fearless, you're just quietly, relentlessly determined.

results.name: Leigh Anne Tuohy
results.subtitle: The Blind Side (2009)
results.description: You are a force of nature who decides what needs to happen and then simply makes it happen, no negotiation required. Loyalty is your whole personality, and anyone who threatens someone you love should be very, very afraid.

results.name: Malorie
results.subtitle: Bird Box (2018)
results.description: You're guarded to the point of being distant, pragmatic almost to a fault, and completely unwilling to let hope in until you absolutely have to. But underneath the cold exterior is someone capable of impossible sacrifice for the people in your care.

results.name: Debbie Ocean
results.subtitle: Ocean's 8 (2018)
results.description: You're always three steps ahead, controlled almost to a fault, and quietly plotting something bigger than anyone around you realizes. Charm is a tool, trust is currency, and you never make a move without an exit plan.

results.name: Margaret Tate
results.subtitle: The Proposal (2009)
results.description: You're brisk, a little terrifying, and allergic to appearing vulnerable in front of literally anyone — until circumstances force you into a fake relationship that thaws you out completely. Underneath the ice-queen exterior is someone who actually wants to be let in.

sources.title: The Proposal quotes and plot summary sources
